data.name: A built-in Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system for fine-grained permission management.
data.value: Sisäänrakennettu roolipohjainen käyttöoikeusjärjestelmä (RBAC) hienojakoista käyttöoikeudenhallintaa varten.

data.name: A complete solution for user authentication and profile management right out of the box.
data.value: Valmis ratkaisu käyttäjätunnistukseen ja profiilin hallintaan heti käyttöönoton yhteydessä.

data.name: A fully responsive layout that looks great on both desktop and mobile devices.
data.value: Täysin responsiivinen ulkoasu, joka näyttää erinomaiselta sekä työpöytä- että mobiililaitteilla.

data.name: A modern, feature-rich, and highly extensible foundation for your next web application.
data.value: Nykyaikainen, ominaisuuksiltaan monipuolinen ja erittäin laajennettava perusta seuraavaa verkkosovellustasi varten.

data.name: A simple tool to convert your Markdown into clean, sanitized HTML.
data.value: Yksinkertainen työkalu Markdown-kielesi muuntamiseksi puhtaaksi, desinfioiduksi HTML:ksi.

data.name: After making this document private, the existing public link will no longer work. Are you sure you want to proceed?
data.value: Kun teet tästä dokumentista yksityisen, olemassa oleva julkinen linkki lakkaa toimimasta. Oletko varma, että haluat jatkaa?

data.name: All dependencies are automatically registered and always available via constructor injection.
data.value: Kaikki riippuvuudet rekisteröidään automaattisesti ja ne ovat aina saatavilla konstruktorin kautta tapahtuvan injektoinnin välityksellä.

data.name: An error occurred. Please try again.
Are you sure you want to make this document private?
data.value: Oletko varma, että haluat tehdä tästä dokumentista yksityisen?

data.name: Automatic, AI-driven translation into 28 languages for global reach.
data.value: Automaattinen, tekoälyavusteinen käännös 28 kielelle maailmanlaajuista saavutettavuutta varten.

data.name: Comes with full Docker support for simplified, consistent, and reliable deployments.
data.value: Tukee täysin Docker-ympäristöä, mikä helpottaa, tehostaa ja luotettavistaa käyttöönottoa.

data.name: Easily switch between providers like SQLite, MySQL, SQL Server, and an in-memory database for testing.
data.value: Vaihda helposti tietokantapalveluntarjoajien, kuten SQLite, MySQL, SQL Server ja testaukseen tarkoitetun muistissa olevan tietokannan, välillä.

data.name: Failed to make document private. Please try again.
data.value: Asiakirjan tekeminen yksityiseksi epäonnistui. Yritä uudelleen.

data.name: Failed to make document public. Please try again.
Features a built-in file management system with automatic avatar compression.
HTML preview will appear here after conversion.
data.value: HTML-esikatselu tulee näkyviin tässä konvertoinnin jälkeen.

data.name: If you make this document private, the existing public link will immediately stop working for everyone.
data.value: Jos teet tästä dokumentista yksityisen, olemassa oleva julkinen linkki lakkaa toimimasta välittömästi kaikille.

data.name: Includes extensive documentation and practical examples to get you started quickly.
data.value: Sisältää laajan dokumentaation ja käytännön esimerkit, joiden avulla pääset nopeasti alkuun.

data.name: Localization
data.value:
<a asp-action="Localize" asp-route-id="@Model.DocumentId" class="btn btn-sm btn-white text-dark shadow-sm" title="@Localizer["Localization"]"> ```
The word "Localize" appears in the action and maybe in the label.
The user says "translate only the sentence or word `Localization`". So we need to output the translation of "Localization". The context is title="@Localizer["Localization"]". So the translation should be the Finnish word for "Localization". So output "Lokalisaatio"? Or "Lokalisointi"? Which is more appropriate for UI title? We need to decide. Let's examine typical usage in Finnish: "Localization" in UI often uses "Lokalisaatio" (maybe because of direct translation of "localization" from English). But "Lokalisointi" is also used. Let's check dictionaries:
- "Localization" = "lokalisaatio". In Finnish, "lokalisaatio" appears in computing contexts: e.g., "lokalisaatio" (noun). For example, "Ohjelman lokalisaatio" is used (program localization). But also "lokalisaatio" is used. "Lokalisointi" is also used: "Ohjelman lokalisointi". I think both are possible, but "lokalisaatio" is more directly borrowed from English "localization". "Lokalisointi" is the process noun derived from localiso? Finnish nouns can be "lokalisaatio" (feminine maybe? Actually "lokalisaatio" ends with "-aatio", which is a typical borrowed noun ending. Also "lokalisaatio" might be a proper term.
Let's see typical translations: In a .NET environment, the resource string might be "Localization". In many UI translations, "Localization" is translated as "Lokalizace"? That's Czech. In Finnish, I think it's "Lokalisaatio". For example, open-source projects have Finnish translation: "Lokalisaatio" appears frequently. Let's search memory: I recall seeing "Lokalisaatio" in UI. Actually "Lokalisaatio" is the term for "Localize" in Finnish .NET? Not sure. Let's check Google "Finnish localization translation". I think "Lokalisointi" is more Finnish typical: "Ohjelman lokalisointi". But "Lokalisaatio" may also be used. Let’s search mental memory: In "Microsoft .NET" documentation Finnish translation (maybe for Visual Studio?), "localization" is "lokalisaatio". I've seen "Lokalisaatio" in some UI. But also "Lokalisointi" is used. There's a minor difference: "Lokalisaatio" is a direct translation of the English word "localization" (from French "localisation"?). "Lokalisointi" is a Finnish derivative.
Let's look up dictionary: "localisation" = "lokalisaatio"? I'm not certain. Could be "lokalisation"? There's also "lokalisaatio" synonyms.
Let’s check typical usage: On the Finnish version of the Wikipedia page "Software localization" is "ohjelmistojen lokalisointi". So it's "Lokalisointi". Indeed the Wikipedia article "Software localization" in Finnish is "Ohjelmistojen lokalisointi". So "Lokalisointi" is correct.
Thus "Localization" -> "Lokalisointi". However "Localization" (the process) can be "Lokalisointi". So we should output "Lokalisointi". Let's confirm via known references: "Localization" - "lokalizacja"? That's Polish. "Lokalisointi" is indeed a Finnish noun. Finnish has a term "lokalisaatio"? Let's check: Finnish uses "lokalisaatio" for "localization"? I'm not sure. There's "lokalisaatio" used in computer science: "lokalisaatio" maybe used in mathematics, but I'm not sure.
Let's think about morphological reasons: The suffix "-onti" in Finnish forms nouns meaning action: e.g., "organisaatio" (organization) from "organise". Similarly "lokalisaatio" (maybe). "Lokalisointi" is from the verb "lokalisoitua"? Finnish verb "lokalisoitua" maybe. Actually "lokalisoitua" means "to become localized". "Lokalisointi" could be derived from "lokalisoiminen"? Honestly both could be plausible. Many Finnish translations use "Lokalisointi". Example: "Open-source ohjelmistojen lokalisointi". Yes, I've seen that.
Thus translation: "Lokalisointi". If we output that, it's likely correct.
But check user request: "Please translate only the sentence or word `Localization` in the paragraph into the target language fi-FI, Suomi (Suomi). ... Human names, Nicknames, Proper nouns, symbols, trademarks, etc. must be output as it is and do not translate them! So please consider if the sentence or word `Localization` is one of them."
"Localization" is not a proper noun. It's a technical term. So we translate.
Thus output: `Lokalisointi`. Possibly capitalizing first letter? Title case: "Lokalisointi". In UI strings, might be "Lokalisointi". That is fine.
Thus output in code block: `Lokalisointi`.
Will they accept? Should we add period? No.
Thus final answer:
``` Lokalisointi ``` </think>
``` Lokalisointi

data.name: Localize
data.value:
Lokalisoi ```
But need to be careful: The user also says: "If you don't understand what you want to translate, please output it as it is!" This is not ambiguous: we know the word "Localize". Finnish translation: "Lokalisoi". That is the verb in imperative form. However, "Localize" could also be a noun "Localize"? No. "Localize" is a verb imperative (command). In Finnish UI you'd use "Lokalisoi". Yes.
Check synonyms: "Localise" in British English is the same. Finnish translation of "localize" is "lokalisoida". Imperative present second person singular is "lokalisoi". However, maybe they want "Lokalisoi". Or "Lokalisoidu"? "Lokalisoi" is correct.
Alternatively, "localize" might be "lokaliso" (if we borrowed English). However, it's more Finnish to use "lokalisoima"? But no, "lokalisoida" is the verb. Imperative singular: "lokalisoi". Let's check Finnish verb: "lokalisoida" is transitive, meaning "to localise (something)". Present third person singular: "lokalisoit" maybe not. Actually "lokalisoida" is a regular verb: infinitive "lokalisoida". Present indicative: "lokalisoit" (you localize). Imperative: "lokalisoikä"? No, imperative second person singular is "lokalisoikä"? Wait, Finnish imperative for second person singular uses "-kä" or "?" The imperative mood forms: "lokalisoikä"? That seems weird. Finnish rarely uses imperative for second person singular; often uses "lokaliso" not sure. Actually, Finnish language doesn't have an imperative form for second person singular; uses present indicative. But UI may use "Lokalisoi" as a command, from "lokalisoida". I think it's okay to output "Lokalisoi". That is the translation.
Thus answer: wrap in triple backticks: ```Lokalisoi```.
Double-check if the user expects translation of the entire line "Localize"? It says "Please translate only the sentence or word `Localize` in the paragraph". So just the word. Output must be only the translation and nothing else. So we must output:
``` Lokalisoi ```
Make sure we follow instructions: wrap in three backticks.
Thus answer. </think>
```Lokalisoi

data.name: Public - Anyone with link
data.value: Julkinen - Ken tahansa, jolla on linkki

data.name: Seamlessly integrate with any OpenID Connect provider, with automatic role synchronization.
data.value: Saatumattomasti integroi minkä tahansa OpenID Connect -palveluntarjoajan kanssa, automaattisen roolisynkronoinnin avulla.

data.name: Share this document publicly with a unique link that anyone can access without logging in.
data.value: Jaa tämä dokumentti julkisesti yksilöllisellä linkillä, jota kuka tahansa voi käyttää ilman kirjautumista.

data.name: Smart dark mode that automatically adapts to the user's system settings.
data.value: Älykäs tumma tila, joka mukautuu automaattisesti käyttäjän järjestelmäasetuksiin.

data.name: The navigation bar automatically adjusts based on user roles and permissions.
data.value: Navigointipalkki sopeutuu automaattisesti käyttäjän roolien ja käyttöoikeuksien perusteella.

data.name: This is a template project, where we start the voyager...
data.value: Tämä on malliprojekti, josta aloitamme matkan...
